activation_results.reason: Input is verbatim the skill's canonical description.
Input matches the skill's stated purpose of plain-language DevOps triage.
User explicitly invokes the skill by its exact name.
SSH key forwarding is a host-config question unrelated to DevOps maturity triage.
Meta question about what skills are; no DevOps triage intent.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Scanned the artefact; none of the listed US spellings appear.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'TL;DR', 'What your app looks like right now'
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings appear as ## sections in the artefact.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Target named 'Tasklet' appears in title and TL;DR; concrete paths like .github/workflows/, README.md, package-lock.json are used.

judge_result.notes: The example artefact satisfies all three judge criteria: it is written in neutral/Australian English with no proscribed US spellings, contains the canonical TL;DR and 'What your app looks like right now' sections, and references a concrete target (Tasklet) plus real file paths rather than placeholders.
